joint_stress_considerations: Use a pain-free joint path and individual setup; persistent joint pain is a substitution trigger, not a progression target.
weekly_set_contribution: 1.0 to mg_chest_sternal; 0.5 default to clearly loaded secondary muscles
load_increase_criteria: All prescribed sets at 10 reps with target RIR and standardized execution for 2 session(s)
rep_increase_criteria: Add one repetition to the lowest-performing eligible set while technique and target RIR remain valid.
set_increase_criteria: Add one weekly set only after at least two weeks without meaningful progression, recovery is acceptable, and current sets retain quality.
regression_criteria: Comparable performance below recent median beyond expected noise for 2 sessions
deload_criteria: Two or more repeated regressions plus fatigue indicators, or widespread multi-exercise decline; do not deload from one noisy session.
technique_quality_criteria: Repeatable setup and joint path; no new compensatory momentum; target muscle remains limiting before pain, grip, cardio, or balance.
range_of_motion_criteria: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM that preserves the intended joint path; ROM must be comparable before load/reps count as progression.
normal_performance_variability: Treat approximately 2-5% e1RM or one rep as possible session noise; use multiple sessions.
stall_definition: No improvement in reps, load, RIR, ROM, or execution across 3 comparable sessions after recovery checks
substitution_triggers: Persistent pain; target muscle not limiting; equipment increments too coarse; repeated technical breakdown; or no progress across a full mesocycle despite recovery and adherence.
suggested_substitutions: Use another exercise in the same movement pattern targeting the same primary muscle with lower joint or stability cost.
cutting_adjustment: Retain load exposure and target RIR; accept slower progression and reduce low-value sets before reducing effort on all sets.
bulking_adjustment: Do not force load jumps from calorie surplus alone; add volume only after performance and recovery support it.
notes: Exercise prescription is inferred mainly from movement-pattern evidence and fatigue/measurement constraints; it is not proof that this exercise is uniquely hypertrophic.

set_increase_criteria: Add one weekly set only after at least two weeks without meaningful progression, recovery is acceptable, and current sets retain quality.
regression_criteria: Comparable performance below recent median beyond expected noise for 2 sessions
deload_criteria: Two or more repeated regressions plus fatigue indicators, or widespread multi-exercise decline; do not deload from one noisy session.
technique_quality_criteria: Repeatable setup and joint path; no new compensatory momentum; target muscle remains limiting before pain, grip, cardio, or balance.
range_of_motion_criteria: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM that preserves the intended joint path; ROM must be comparable before load/reps count as progression.
normal_performance_variability: One repetition or one small machine increment may be noise; use the defined confirmation window.
stall_definition: No improvement in reps, load, RIR, ROM, or execution across 3 comparable sessions after recovery checks
substitution_triggers: Persistent pain; target muscle not limiting; equipment increments too coarse; repeated technical breakdown; or no progress across a full mesocycle despite recovery and adherence.
suggested_substitutions: Use another exercise in the same movement pattern targeting the same primary muscle with lower joint or stability cost.
cutting_adjustment: Retain load exposure and target RIR; accept slower progression and reduce low-value sets before reducing effort on all sets.
bulking_adjustment: Do not force load jumps from calorie surplus alone; add volume only after performance and recovery support it.
notes: Exercise prescription is inferred mainly from movement-pattern evidence and fatigue/measurement constraints; it is not proof that this exercise is uniquely hypertrophic.
